chapters.caption: Raskolnikov stands in the pawnbroker's apartment, axe in hand, as Lizaveta unexpectedly enters and discovers the bloodied body of her sister on the floor.
Sonya kneels at the bedside of her dying father Marmeladov, surrounded by family members and a priest administering last rites.
Investigator Porfiry Petrovich sits at his desk laughing with theatrical exuberance during his psychological cat-and-mouse interrogation of Raskolnikov.
Sonya stands in her dimly lit room, hands raised in an imploring gesture, as the dark silhouette of Raskolnikov listens and a Bible lies open on the table between them.
Svidrigailov stands alone in the dark before a stone archway on the night before his suicide, a stray dog prowling in the shadows behind him.

chapters.reason: No qualifying public domain illustration depicting Raskolnikov in Siberian penal servitude or his spiritual renewal with Sonya could be found after searching Wikimedia Commons, Library of Congress, and multiple gallery archives.
